title: Timing Alpha

content.statement: The same move can be brilliant or stupid depending on when you do it.
content.question: Is right now early, late, or exactly on time for your model in this market? What proof do you have, not gut feel?
content.equation: TA = Actual Return from Timing - Expected Return from Average Timing
sop: List the market signals that would indicate too-early (no demand, no awareness) versus right-on-time (rising demand, competitors entering).
Check which signals you're actually seeing right now versus which you're assuming.
If evidence points to early, find a smaller, cheaper way to stay in the game until the timing signals flip.
Rate your Actual Return from Timing and your Expected Return from Average Timing 1-10 (see calculation, page 6)
Reassess timing every quarter, being early isn't permanent, but staying there too long is a choice.
content.explainer: Early is often indistinguishable from wrong. Late is usually fatal. Timing is a skill, not luck.
questions: Is right now early, late, or exactly on time for your model in this market? What proof do you have, not gut feel?
What would have to be true for too-early to actually mean ahead-of-everyone-else?
What signal, if it appeared tomorrow, would tell you it's time to move faster, not slower?

definitions.def: Extra return you get beyond what you'd expect from an average, unremarkable execution of the same idea.

content.equation_example: Rate your Actual Return from acting at this specific time 1-10, and your Expected Return from acting at an average or typical time 1-10. Actual Return 8, Expected Return from Average Timing 5 → TA = 8 - 5 = 3. A positive TA means your timing is adding real advantage; a negative TA means the timing itself is working against you, regardless of how good the idea is.
content.closing_statement: Prove your timing with evidence, not conviction.

caption:
Principle Nº 39: Timing Alpha

The same move can be brilliant or stupid depending on when you do it. Early is often indistinguishable from wrong.

Ask yourself: Is right now early, late, or exactly on time for your model in this market? What proof do you have, not gut feel?
